title: Robert  Hayhoe
content:
Partner | Lead Charities and Non-Profits
People.ContactEmail rhayhoe@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2006
- University of British Columbia
- Osgoode Hall Law School
Robert delivers strategic, high-value legal counsel to sophisticated tax-exempt organizations navigating complex regulatory, cross-border, and governance considerations.
Robert Hayhoe is a partner in McCarthy Tétrault’s Toronto and Vancouver offices and leads the firm’s Charities and Non-Profits practice. Recognized by Chambers and Partners for his top-tier counsel, he is widely regarded as one of Canada’s leading advisors to complex and high-profile charities, not-for-profits, and foundations. A Certified Specialist in Tax Law by the Law Society of Ontario, Robert brings extensive experience in Canadian tax and regulatory matters, with a strong cross-border focus.
Clients trust Robert for his unmatched ability to navigate the intricate regulatory and tax landscape governing tax-exempt entities—particularly when the stakes are high and the issues are cross-border, reputational, or strategically significant. Whether structuring international activities, managing foreign expenditures, or responding to regulatory scrutiny, Robert delivers practical, forward-thinking solutions that anticipate risk, unlock opportunity, and align with the expectations of preeminent global institutions.
Consistently ranked Band 1 for Charities/Non-profits since Chambers and Partners began covering the practice area in 2017, his distinguished expertise, combined with a proven track record of advising multinational organizations, makes him the go-to counsel for clients seeking the clarity, compliance, and confidence to operate seamlessly in Canada and beyond.
Robert’s practice complements our private client tax planning and trusts and estates groups, offering integrated solutions to ultra-high-net-worth individuals and public companies with philanthropic initiatives. He has led national legal teams on complex mandates such as mergers of charitable organizations, the development of social enterprise structures, and the navigation of advocacy-related legal frameworks—always with a focus on aligning legal strategy with mission and impact.
A recognized thought leader, Robert is the author of Charities Taxation, Policy and Practice , a leading textbook in the field, and is a regular speaker at national tax and charity law conferences, having presented at more than 100 events since 2008. His insights are sought after by peers and clients alike, and his leadership has helped shape the legal landscape for tax-exempt organizations in Canada.
Robert is active in numerous professional organizations, including the Canadian Tax Foundation, the International Fiscal Association, and the Society of Trust and Estate Practitioners (TEP), among others. He also engages in cross-border legal policy through roles with the Canadian and American Bar Associations. In addition to his professional memberships, Robert contributes to the Canadian Tax Journal , the International Journal of Not-for-Profit Law , the Canadian Bar Review and the Exempt Organization Tax Review .
Outside of work, Robert serves on the board of the Lake Joseph Yacht Club.
Robert obtained an LLM (Taxation) from Osgoode Hall Law School at York University in 2000 and received his LLB from the University of British Columbia in 1995. He holds an Honours BA in Economics and International Relations from Trinity College at the University of Toronto. He was called to the Bar of Ontario in 1998 and to the Bar of British Columbia in 2006.
